title: System task function grammar isn't the same as the standard
body:
The Verilog and SystemVerilog standard specify `system_tf_identifier` `system_function_identifier` and `system_task_identifier` as `$[ a-zA-Z0-9_$ ]{ [ a-zA-Z0-9_$ ] }` whereas the lexer accepts `$[ a-zA-Z_$ ]{ [ a-zA-Z0-9_$ ] }`.
I do not have a *working* test file to submit because PLI is not properly implemented and I couldn't get working the system function import with DPI.
If `file.v` contains
```
module a;
    initial $0 ();
endmodule
```
running `verilator --lint-only file.v` gives
```
%Error: file.v:2:11: syntax error, unexpected INTEGER NUMBER
    2 |  initial $0 ();
      |           ^
%Error: Exiting due to 1 error(s)
```
instead of
```
%Error: ../file.v:2:10: Unsupported or unknown PLI call: '$0'
    2 |  initial $0 ();
      |          ^~
%Error: Exiting due to 1 error(s)
        ... See the manual at https://verilator.org/verilator_doc.html for more assistance.
```
